name: AutEng Markdown Publishing MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for publishing markdown documents as public share links with Mermaid diagram support. Enables AI agents to publish markdown content — including diagrams, documentation, and reports — as shareable web pages, bridging AI-generated text output to shareable published content.
use_cases: Publishing AI-generated documentation as shareable web links from documentation agents
Sharing architecture diagrams and Mermaid flowcharts from technical writing agents
Creating shareable reports from AI analysis agents without needing a web host
Converting agent-generated markdown content into instant public web pages
not_for: High-volume documentation publishing at enterprise scale
Complex web applications beyond markdown + diagrams
best_when: An AI agent generates markdown documentation or diagrams and needs to share the output as a web link — rather than sending raw markdown text that requires the recipient to render it.
avoid_when: Publishing confidential or sensitive information — share links may be publicly accessible. Also: Mermaid diagram rendering requires recipients' browsers, not needed for simple text.

auth.notes: AutEng account required. Published links — verify these are public or private before use. API key or account credentials for publishing.

pricing.notes: Emerging publishing service — pricing not publicly documented.

known_agent_gotchas: Published links may be publicly accessible — do not publish confidential agent outputs
Markdown content passes through AutEng servers for rendering
Mermaid diagram rendering depends on AutEng's client-side rendering
Link persistence: verify links don't expire unexpectedly
Emerging service — reliability not yet proven at production scale
agent_readiness.security_notes: Published content may be public. Sensitive agent outputs must not be published. Markdown content processed by AutEng servers. Verify link access controls.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public document publishing risk. Content processed by third party. Sensitive information must not be published. Link accessibility control required.
